---
title: "stan_intro"
author: "esm"
format: html
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

## Intro to Stan

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidybayes)
library(bayesplot)
library(posterior)
library(cmdstanr)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r}
set.seed(42)
n <- 30
d <- tibble(
  x = rnorm(n = n, mean = 0, sd = 1),
  y = 0 + 1*x + rnorm(n = n, mean = 0, sd = 1)
)

# Ask cmdstan to compile stan code
m1 <- cmdstan_model("models/gaussian.stan")
# Fit the model to data
f1 <- m1$sample(
  data = list(
    N = n, # Number of obs
    y = d$y # Outcome (no predictors here)
  ),
  iter_warmup = 500, # warmup samples, stan does autotuning of stepsizes etc.
  iter_sampling = 1000,# actual inference samples
  chains = 4
)

print(f1) #print summary

mcmc_trace(f1$draws(c("mu", "sigma")))


f1 %>% # plot posterior mu, sigma
  gather_draws(mu, sigma) %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(x = .value, color = .variable)
  )+
  stat_halfeye()+
  facet_wrap(~.variable, scale = "free")

f1 %>% 
  spread_draws(mu, ndraws = 50) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_point(data = d, aes(x,y))+
  geom_abline(aes(intercept = mu, slope = 0, group = .draw), alpha = 0.2)
```

once we have the simple model working, we can increase the complexity
```{r}
m1b <- cmdstan_model("models/gaussian_linear.stan")


f1b <- m1b$sample(
  data = list(
    N = n, 
    y = d$y,
    x = d$x
  ),
  iter_warmup = 500,
  iter_sampling = 1000,
  refresh = 500
)

f1b %>% 
  gather_draws(beta0, beta1, sigma) %>% 
  ggplot(
    aes(x = .value, color = .variable)
  )+
  stat_halfeye()+
  facet_wrap(~.variable, scale = "free")

f1b %>% 
  spread_draws(beta0, beta1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(beta0, beta1))+
  geom_point()+
  coord_equal()

# Over samples from the posterior; notice we are not doing any stats on the posterior, we are just plotting random samples
f1b %>% 
  spread_draws(beta0, beta1, ndraws = 50) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_point(data = d, aes(x,y))+
  geom_abline(aes(intercept = beta0, slope = beta1, group = .draw), alpha = 0.2)
```




example of poorly sampling model
```{r}
m2 <- cmdstan_model("week3/models/funnel_centered.stan")
f2 <- m2$sample(
  data = list(),
  iter_warmup = 500, # warmup samples, stan does autotuning of stepsizes etc.
  iter_sampling = 1000, # actual inference samples
  chains = 4
)

f2

mcmc_trace(f2$draws(c("y", "x")))

draws <- f2$draws()

df <- as_draws_df(draws) |>
  transmute(
    chain = factor(.chain),
    iteration = .iteration,
    y = y,
    x1 = `x[1]`
  )
boundary_df <- tibble(
  y = seq(min(df$y), max(df$y), length.out = 300),
  upper = 2 * exp(y / 2),
  lower = -2 * exp(y / 2)
)

ggplot(df, aes(x = y, y = x1, color = chain)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, size = 1) +
  geom_line(
    data = boundary_df,
    aes(x = y, y = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = boundary_df,
    aes(x = y, y = lower),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Centered Neal's funnel",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines show approximate ±2 exp(y / 2) boundaries",
    x = "y",
    y = "x[1]",
    color = "Chain"
  )
```

```{r}
m2 <- cmdstan_model("week3/models/funnel_uncentered.stan")
f2 <- m2$sample(
  data = list(),
  iter_warmup = 500, # warmup samples, stan does autotuning of stepsizes etc.
  iter_sampling = 1000, # actual inference samples
  chains = 4
)

f2

mcmc_trace(f2$draws(c("y", "x")))

draws <- f2$draws()

df <- as_draws_df(draws) |>
  transmute(
    chain = factor(.chain),
    iteration = .iteration,
    y = y,
    x1 = `x[1]`
  )
boundary_df <- tibble(
  y = seq(min(df$y), max(df$y), length.out = 300),
  upper = 2 * exp(y / 2),
  lower = -2 * exp(y / 2)
)

ggplot(df, aes(x = y, y = x1, color = chain)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, size = 1) +
  geom_line(
    data = boundary_df,
    aes(x = y, y = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = boundary_df,
    aes(x = y, y = lower),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1,
    color = "black",
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Centered Neal's funnel",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines show approximate ±2 exp(y / 2) boundaries",
    x = "y",
    y = "x[1]",
    color = "Chain"
  )
```



The importance of prior and posterior predictive checks

```{r}
m3 <- cmdstan_model("models/prior_and_posterior_checks.stan")

n3 <- 10
d3 <- tibble(y = rbinom(n = 1, size = n3, prob = 0.75))

f3a <- m3$sample(
  data = list(
    N = n3, 
    y = d3$y,
    prior_p_sigma = 5
  ),
  iter_warmup = 500,
  iter_sampling = 1000,
  refresh = 500
)

f3b <- m3$sample(
  data = list(
    N = n3, 
    y = d3$y,
    prior_p_sigma = 1
  ),
  iter_warmup = 500,
  iter_sampling = 1000,
  refresh = 500
)


pa <- f3a %>% 
  spread_draws(y_sim) %>% 
  count(y_sim) %>% 
  mutate(prob = n / sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = y_sim, y = prob)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", fill = "lightgrey") +
  labs(
    y = "Probability mass",
    x = str_glue("N successes out of {n3}"),
    title = "N(0, 5) prior on p",
    subtitle = str_glue("y ~ binomial({n3}, Phi(p))")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 0.4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n3)

pb <- f3b %>% 
  spread_draws(y_sim) %>% 
  count(y_sim) %>% 
  mutate(prob = n / sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = y_sim, y = prob)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", fill = "lightgrey") +
  labs(
    y = "Probability mass",
    x = str_glue("N successes out of {n3}"),
    title = "N(0, 1) prior on p",
    subtitle = str_glue("y ~ binomial({n3}, Phi(p))")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 0.4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n3)

pb+pa

pbpos <- f3b %>% 
  spread_draws(y_rep) %>% 
  count(y_rep) %>% 
  mutate(prob = n / sum(n)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = y_rep, y = prob)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    y = "Probability mass",
    x = str_glue("N successes out of {n3}"),
    title = "N(0, 1) prior on p",
    subtitle = str_glue("y ~ binomial({n3}, Phi(p))")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 0.4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n3)


pbpos



pmf_draws <- f3b %>% 
  spread_draws(p) %>% 
  crossing(y_rep = 0:n3) %>% 
  mutate(prob = dbinom(y_rep, size = n3, prob = pnorm(p)))

ggplot(pmf_draws, aes(x = y_rep, y = prob)) +
  stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95) +
  labs(
    y = "Posterior predictive probability mass",
    x = str_glue("N successes out of {n3}"),
    title = "Posterior predictive PMF",
    subtitle = str_glue("y ~ binomial({n3}, Phi(p))")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:n3)
```
